title: Extraordinary
description: What happens when you've been stuck behind a mask for so long that you don't even know what your real voice sounds like? Elaine has spent too long being seen and not heard - and sometimes barely that. We meet Elaine trapped in the hold of a toxic relationship, and losing herself in the noise of the world around her. Straight-laced and a sucker for rules, she yearns for something more. Find out what happens when she meets the effervescent Clementine. Can she break familial cycles and ensure she finds her own voice? A new play written by Gemma Webber and co-directed by Gemma Webber and Robin Hodges. Content Warnings: Mentions of bereavement, and themes of coercive control and violence.

reviews.excerpt: Genre: Dance and Movement Theatre Difficult to categorise, this unsettling, captivating and ethereal piece is a melange of physical theatre, dance and puppetry. Probably more suited for an adult audience, it delivers its stygian story and message in a hypnotic potion of movement, noise and light. A truly unforgettable experience.
